function_v0.doc:
Return the current admin address.

# Errors
* [`OracleError::NotInitialized`] if the contract has not been set up.
function_v0.name: get_admin

function_v0.doc:
Return the [`PRICE_PRECISION`]-scaled price of `asset`.

# Arguments
* `asset` \xe2\x80\x93 The SEP-41 token address to query.

# Errors
* [`OracleError::NotInitialized`] if the contract has not been set up.
* [`OracleError::PriceNotFound`]  if no price has been set for `asset`.
function_v0.name: get_price

function_v0.doc:
Transfer admin rights to `new_admin`.

# Auth
Current admin must authorize this call.

# Errors
* [`OracleError::NotInitialized`] if the contract has not been set up.
function_v0.name: set_admin

function_v0.doc:
Set (or update) the price of `asset`.

The price must be expressed in [`PRICE_PRECISION`]-scaled units.
To set "1 USDC = 1.0 base unit" pass `price = 10_000_000`.

# Arguments
* `asset` \xe2\x80\x93 The SEP-41 token address whose price is being set.
* `price` \xe2\x80\x93 PRECISION-scaled price. Must be > 0.

# Auth
Admin must authorize this call.

# Errors
* [`OracleError::NotInitialized`]   if the contract has not been set up.
* [`OracleError::NotAuthorized`]    if caller is not the admin.
* [`OracleError::NonPositivePrice`] if `price <= 0`.
function_v0.name: set_price

function_v0.doc:
Return a map of prices for all requested assets in a single call.

This is a convenience batch-read that avoids multiple round-trips.

# Arguments
* `assets` \xe2\x80\x93 List of SEP-41 token addresses to query.

# Errors
* [`OracleError::PriceNotFound`] if any asset in the list has no price.
function_v0.name: get_prices

function_v0.doc:
Constructor \xe2\x80\x94 runs atomically with `CreateContract`, cannot be front-run.

The deployer supplies the initial `admin` address. Because this executes
in the same transaction as contract creation, no attacker can race ahead
and claim admin rights.

# Arguments
* `admin` \xe2\x80\x93 The address that will be permitted to call [`set_price`].
function_v0.name: __constructor

function_v0.doc: Return the configured maximum allowed price age in ledgers.
function_v0.name: get_max_age_ledgers

function_v0.doc:
Set the maximum allowed age of stored prices in ledgers.

`max_age_ledgers` must be \xe2\x89\xa5 1; zero would permanently disable the
staleness guard, accepting arbitrarily old prices.

# Errors
* [`OracleError::InvalidMaxAge`] if `max_age_ledgers == 0`.
function_v0.name: set_max_age_ledgers

udt_error_enum_v0.doc: Errors emitted by the Oracle contract.
udt_error_enum_v0.name: OracleError

cases.doc: No price has been set for the requested asset.
cases.name: PriceNotFound

cases.doc: Price exists but is older than the allowed freshness threshold.
cases.name: StalePrice

cases.doc: `max_age_ledgers` must be at least 1; zero would disable the staleness\ncheck entirely, meaning any arbitrarily old price would be accepted.
cases.name: InvalidMaxAge

void_v0.doc: Maximum acceptable age for price data in ledgers.
void_v0.name: MaxAgeLedgers

tuple_v0.doc: Price of an asset, PRICE_PRECISION-scaled \xe2\x80\x94 persistent storage.
tuple_v0.name: Price
